calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute; Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Gregory Tuss
Off Calendar Dec-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA BOARD OF ACOUNTANCY-DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER AFFAIRS' Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute.

Respondent's "motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute" is granted; no timely opposition filed.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay California Board Of Accountancy Decision And Order Brought Against Petitioner Michael Robinson
Off Calendar Jan-12-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ANGELE K. CICALA, Esq. of Gordon & Rees Scully Mansukhani (Tel: 415-986-5900) for petitioner Michael Robinson;
Atty. GREGORY TUSS, Esq, of California Attorney General's Office (Tel: 415-510-3435) for respondent California Board of Accountancy, Department of Consumer Affairs.

HEARING RE: PETITIONER MICHAEL ROBINSON's Motion To Stay California Board Of Accountancy Decision And Order Brought Against Petitioner Michael Robinson.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification, as follows:

Petitioner's motion for a stay (including the petition to revoke probation) is denied. First, petitioner is obligated to show that the public interest will not suffer and respondent is unlikely to prevail on the merits because respondent (the agency) adopted the administrative law judge's decision in its entirety. (See CCP 1094.5(h)(2).) Petitioner fails to demonstrate that respondent is unlikely to prevail on the merits. Indeed, it is undisputed that petitioner failed to obtain the required peer review. (Petition, par. 44.) Second, petitioner has acted with unclean hands with regard to his stay request. (See Kendall-Jackson Winery, Ltd. v. Superior Court (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 970, 979; Elliott v. Contractors' State License Board (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 1048, 1055). Petitioner filed his petition on March 27, 2019. Petitioner did not diligently seek a stay of the administrative decision. Rather, petitioner ignored the probation requirements and only sought a stay after ignoring those requirements for years and being faced with a petition for revocation. (Murata Decl.) Under the circumstances, it would be inequitable to issue the stay.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
